programs.name: Computers: Web Design
programs.description: Have you ever thought about designing your very own webpage? Combining technical elements and artistic design, instruction will focus on the application of basic web design standards. Participants should have basic computer skills and understand how to navigate the internet.Equipment: Use of computers vary by location, please check ahead. You may bring a laptop.

programs.name: Conceptual Art Mixed-Media Exploration
programs.description: Explore personal narratives, emotions, and ideas through mixed media collage and conceptual art practices. Using layering, found materials, drawing, text and experimental techniques, students will learn how to translate inner experiences into meaningful and visually engaging compositions. The emphasis is on process, intuition, self-expression and the creative use of a variety of materials. No prior art experience is required.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Computers: Web Design
programs.description: Have you ever thought about designing your very own webpage? Combining technical elements and artistic design, instruction will focus on the application of basic web design standards. Participants should have basic computer skills and understand how to navigate the internet.Equipment: Use of computers vary by location, please check ahead. You may bring a laptop.

programs.name: Conceptual Art Mixed-Media Exploration
programs.description: Explore personal narratives, emotions, and ideas through mixed media collage and conceptual art practices. Using layering, found materials, drawing, text and experimental techniques, students will learn how to translate inner experiences into meaningful and visually engaging compositions. The emphasis is on process, intuition, self-expression and the creative use of a variety of materials. No prior art experience is required.

programs.name: Cooking
programs.description: Let’s get creative in the kitchen and have some fun. In this introduction to cooking, you will learn about healthy foods, the importance of safety and practice basic skills like mixing and measuring with easy-to-follow recipes that taste great.The City of Toronto and its staff cannot guarantee that the foods or supplies within its cooking programs are free of allergens. Notify staff at your location of any food allergies or dietary restrictions you may have.

programs.name: Cooking: Baking
programs.description: Whether you are making breads, desserts or pastries; something savoury or sweet, this activity will help you reach new culinary delights. Come learn the basics of cooking and how to prepare delicious and nutritious recipes inspired by Canada's Food Guide. From measuring and mixing ingredients to food safety, you will gain confidence in the kitchen. The City of Toronto and its staff cannot guarantee that the foods or supplies within its cooking programs are free of allergens. Notify staff at your location of any food allergies or dietary restrictions you may have.

For more information on volunteer opportunities, contact your local community centre.

programs.name: Crafts: Diamond Dots
programs.description: Create dazzling artwork, one dot at a time! Using tiny, sparkly "diamonds," you'll place each one onto a pre-printed adhesive canvas to build beautiful, shimmering designs. Diamond dotting is a fun and relaxing activity that encourages patience, focus, and creativity. Whether you're a beginner or a seasoned crafter, you'll love watching your masterpiece come to life, dot by dot!

programs.name: Dance: Jazz
programs.description: This modern style of dance is all about fast footwork and lively movements. From kicks and lunges to leaps and gallops, learn proper technique through various exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Dance: Jazz
programs.description: This modern style of dance is all about fast footwork and lively movements. From kicks and lunges to leaps and gallops, learn proper technique through various exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Dance: Jazz with Recital
programs.description: Learn basic jazz positions, postures and proper technique through various exercises and engaging choreography. This is an 18 week class that ends with a recital.

programs.name: Dance: Line Dance
programs.description: Are you ready to step up your dance skills with line dancing? Using basic steps to create repetitive combinations, you'll learn moves like the shuffle, grapevine, coaster step and mambo step through various exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Dance: Line Dance
programs.description: Are you ready to step up your dance skills with line dancing? Using basic steps to create repetitive combinations, you'll learn moves like the shuffle, grapevine, coaster step and mambo step through various exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Dance: Soca
programs.description: Caribbean culture is on display in this style of dance. With a focus on fluid hip movements, you will learn basic footwork through a variety of exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Drama
programs.description: Here's an early introduction to the stage and performance art. Through dramatic play and skits learn to express emotion with your body, face and voice, to improve your performing arts skills.

programs.name: Drawing & Painting Studio
programs.description: Practice  drawing and painting from life through still life and floral compositions in  this instructor-led studio course. Learn how to simplify shapes, organize  colours, and capture what you see with real time demonstrations that address  real questions with ongoing guidance and feedback in one-on-one consultation  with the instructor.

programs.name: Drawing and Painting
programs.description: Explore your creativity through pencils, pastels, acrylic paint and watercolours. Learn about and practice skills including sketching, shading, brush strokes, use of colour and texture.

programs.name: Drawing and Painting
programs.description: Explore your creativity through pencils, pastels, acrylic paint and watercolours. Learn about and practice skills including sketching, shading, brush strokes, use of colour and texture.

programs.name: Drawing From the Round
programs.description: Explore drawing basics including portraiture through working with casts  and photographs to improve your drawing. Get hands-on with casts and  photographs as you nail down proportions, head layout and facial feature  placement. Use light logic to bring surface forms to life through a mix of  lectures, demo drawings, and plenty of drawing time in class. Experiment with  one graphite and one charcoal method on different types of paper, to create two  take home projects. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Embroidery
programs.description: Come get an introduction to this handy skill. Learn how to read a pattern, cut material, stich by hand or use a sewing machine with the aim of completing a simple project.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Ultra Youth Swim 1
programs.description: Build confidence in the water and improve your swimming skills. Learn front and back swim, front, side and back glides, going underwater, breathing effectively and staying afloat in deep water. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 to 50 metres.

programs.name: Ultra Youth Swim 1
programs.description: Build confidence in the water and improve your swimming skills. Learn front and back swim, front, side and back glides, going underwater, breathing effectively and staying afloat in deep water. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 to 50 metres.

programs.name: Ultra Youth Swim 1
programs.description: Build confidence in the water and improve your swimming skills. Learn front and back swim, front, side and back glides, going underwater, breathing effectively and staying afloat in deep water. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 to 50 metres.

programs.name: Ultra Youth Swim 2
programs.description: Build confidence in deep water and improve your swimming skills. Develop front and back crawl, elementary backstroke and staying afloat in water. programs.name: Ultra Youth Swim 3
programs.description: Build endurance in the water and improve your swimming skills. Develop entries, surface dives and stroke improvement. Learn to support an object in the water. Work towards a continuous swim of 100 to 300 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Youth 2.

programs.name: Volleyball: Instructional

programs.name: After-School Recreation Care (ARC)
programs.description: Are you seeking recreational care at the end of the school day? After School Recreation Care (ARC) offers outdoor play, crafts, sports, games and physical literacy. Participants will receive homework help, nutritional education and a snack while focusing on social interactions and wellness.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness: Deep
programs.description: Want a deep-water workout that includes cardio, endurance, flexibility and strength-building? With the support of belts and noodles, this activity focuses on combining movement and staying afloat in deep water. Participants must be comfortable in deep water and be able to perform a facility swim test.It is free to reserve a spot in this activity, fees may apply at the time of check in. Please arrive 10 minutes before your scheduled start time to check in with staff and pay for this swim. If you do not arrive within 5 minutes of your start time, your spot will be given to someone in the walk-in line. A printed receipt or a mobile screenshot is required at check in.Participants are permitted to bring their own personal equipment.To cancel, visit your online account, speak with community centre staff, or contact Client Services at 416-396-7378 during regular business hours.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness: Shallow
programs.description: Want a shallow water workout that includes cardio, endurance, flexibility and strength building? With the support of noodles, this activity allows you to focus on movement in the shallow-end of the pool. It is free to reserve a spot in this activity, fees may apply at the time of check in. Please arrive 10 minutes before your scheduled start time to check in with staff and pay for this swim. If you do not arrive within 5 minutes of your start time, your spot will be given to someone in the walk-in line. A printed receipt or a mobile screenshot is required at check in.Participants are permitted to bring their own personal equipment.To cancel, visit your online account, speak with community centre staff, or contact Client Services at 416-396-7378 during regular business hours.

programs.name: Bronze Cross
programs.description: Bronze Cross begins the transition from lifesaving to lifeguarding. Candidates strengthen and expand their lifesaving skills and begin to apply the principles and techniques of active surveillance in aquatic facilities. Bronze Cross emphasizes the importance of teamwork and communication in preventing and responding to aquatic emergencies. Bronze Cross is a prerequisite for advanced training in the Society’s National Lifeguard and leadership certification programs. Candidates must be able to swim 400 metres in 11 minutes or less by the course exam date. This course is generally offered in an extended nine-class format.Prerequisite: Bronze Medallion and Emergency First Aid with CPR-B (current or expired) or Standard First Aid with CPR-C  from an approved provider.

Bronze Cross is a prerequisite for advanced training in the Society’s National Lifeguard and leadership certification programs. Candidates must be able to swim 400 metres in 11 minutes or less by the course exam date. This course is generally offered in an extended nine-class format. Prerequisite: Bronze Medallion and Emergency First Aid with CPR-B (current or expired) or Standard First Aid with CPR-C  from an approved provider.Equipment: Bring your copy of the Canadian Lifesaving manual received in Bronze Medallion.

Candidates must be able to swim 400 metres in 11 minutes or less by the course exam date. programs.name: Bronze Medallion and Emergency First Aid (CPR B)
programs.description: Bronze Medallion challenges the candidate both mentally and physically. Judgment, knowledge, skill and fitness – the four components of water rescue – form the basis of Bronze Medallion training. Candidates acquire the assessment and problem-solving skills needed to make good decisions in, on, and around the water. Bronze Medallion is a prerequisite for assistant lifeguard training in Bronze Cross. Candidates must be able to swim 400 metres in 12 minutes or less by the course exam date. The Emergency First Aid (CPR-B) certification teaches victim assessment, CPR and addressing choking, as well as first aid treatment of external bleeding, heart attack, stroke and wound and burns. This course is generally offered in an extended nine-class format. There will be a in-person exam on week 10.Prerequisite: Minimum age is 13 years old or Bronze Star. Candidates with Bronze Star, that are under 13 years of age, can register for this course by calling 416-396-7378 or visiting an in-person registration site.

programs.name: Bronze Medallion, Cross & Standard First Aid (CPR C)
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. This is an intensive program designed for strong swimmers. The Bronze Medallion certification teaches how to manage complex water rescues through the development of physical fitness and judgment skills. The Bronze Cross certification teaches the difference between lifesaving and lifeguarding, the principles of emergency procedures and teamwork. The Standard First Aid (CPR C) certification provides comprehensive first aid training that covers spinal injuries, heat or cold injuries, bone and joint injuries, abdominal and chest injuries, burns, medical emergencies, and automated external defibrillator usage. Candidates must be able to swim 400 metres in 11 minutes or less by the course exam date.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 13 years old or Bronze Star. Candidates with Bronze Star, that are under 13 years of age, can register for this course by calling 416-396-7378 or visiting an in-person registration site.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Counsellor in Training
programs.description: Becoming a camp counsellor is a once-in-a-lifetime experience. In this camp, you will learn about program planning and working with campers in an interactive environment. You will also get the opportunity to do a supervised practical placement in a City of Toronto summer camp and receive a certificate upon completion.As this is a registered activity, participants will not receive credit for volunteer hours. For more information on volunteer opportunities, contact your local community centre.

With a focus on developing new and more advanced skills, you will enjoy creative, active and interactive activities such as storytelling, theme-based games and crafts, music and physical activity, while having fun with new friends.

programs.name: Recreation Discovery with Caregiver
programs.description: Embark on a recreational discovery with a focus on developing skills through creative, active and interactive activities. Programming will include storytelling, theme-based games, crafts, music and physical activity, while having fun with new friends. A caregiver must actively participate in this activity.

programs.name: Future Lifeguards
programs.description: Prepare to be a future lifeguard in this free activity. Here, swimmers will continue to develop the skills they need to successfully complete Bronze level courses, which are a requirement to become a lifeguard, as well as be encouraged to apply for City of Toronto recreation employment with the support of City staff.Prerequisite: You must be able to demonstrate 70% completion of the Future Lifeguard screening on week one of the program (Ultra 6/7 swimming ability). Candidates who do not meet this requirement will be referred to other City of Toronto swim programming.
